name: Chocolate Peanut Butter Cupcakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Grease the bottoms of 12 muffin cups, or line them with paper baking cups.
3. In a large bowl, mix together the Bisquick, sugar, egg, water/milk, and peanut butter. Beat vigorously for about 30 seconds until well combined.
4. Fill each prepared muffin cup about two-thirds full with the batter.
5. Bake in the preheated oven for about 15 minutes, or until the cupcakes are golden brown.
6. Allow the cupcakes to cool completely on a wire rack.
7. In a small saucepan over low heat, melt the chocolate and margarine together. Stir in the powdered sugar, vanilla, and hot water until smooth.
8. Spread about one teaspoon of the melted chocolate mixture over the top of each cooled cupcake.
9. Dip each cupcake into the chopped peanuts.
From the Kitchen of: Gary & Margie Hartford, Eugene, OR (1:152/19)
Taken from: Bisquick side panel
(1988)
